summary:
Orbital involvement by non-Hodgkin's lymphoma primarily involves B cells, notably MALT subtypes; this contrasts significantly against less frequent occurrences involving different cellular origins like T- or NK-cells within these structures. Agent1 reasoned based on multiple references indicating a strong preference for B-cell malignancies over alternatives when considering specific ocular manifestations due to their higher incidence rates documented clinically. To condense it into two sentences while focusing only on critical aspects related directly back towards addressing our core query about identifying the primary etiology observed anatomically,

The most prevalent type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma found in orbits consists mainly of B-cell varieties including MALT subtype cases reflecting high reported incidences relative to alternative histological classifications impacting those areas more sparingly. This conclusion stems fundamentally because numerous studies highlight significant predominance trends favoring particular blood-derived immune system components manifesting pathologically there
